text: The app’s battery consumption has become completely out of control. Even during basic background playback, my phone overheats and the battery drains at an alarming rate. ​As a Premium subscriber, I pay for a seamless experience and expect to be shielded from these kinds of severe performance flaws. Having my phone's battery decimated just by listening to music completely ruins the service. ​Please look into this bug and let me know when a fix or update will be deployed.

author: Keydalis

text: If you can't afford premium, don't bother. With premium, it's amazing. But if you're broke like me, it's awful. All the songs are on shuffle and you have to pay to play your playlist in order. If your playlist is short with the hack of listening to the songs you actually want to, they add random songs. It wouldn't be an issue if you could skip it without getting FOUR ADS per 2 skips. You gets SO MANY ADS per every few songs and I can't skip them ever. There is more ads than music. ALSO this part drives me so mad the most: You can't rewind the song or go back to your favorite part in the song. If you want to listen to a song again, you have to click it, which will most likely just give you an ad. If you listen to a song just because of one part, don't do it on Spotify. It's a really great music app IF you have premium, otherwise, YouTube is infinitely better.

text: Not enough products for the cost. I initially joined and was more than happy to pay the sub $10 fee per month and then when they decided to charge 4 to 5 dollars more, I ended my service. Since then they have offered me less money per month, if I’m willing to jump back on board, I can apparently cancel anytime, and they’re offering it for a ridiculously low rate for a couple of teasing months. Why not just make it $10 a month instead of being greedy and wanting more. It’s not like they are giving me better service or something I didn’t have before for more money.
